attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from positions 3rd-7th wheel in final 600m, relying on optimal positioning rather than early acceleration or long-distance efforts.
team_tactics: Team provides lead-out train positioning MARICHKA optimally in top 7 positions entering final 600m, absorbing risk (CARTIER crash noted) to deliver protected sprint launch.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where positioning and pure speed determine outcome; explicitly uncertain about defending on mountainous terrain.
vulnerability: Mountains expose limitations - rider himself acknowledges uncertainty about defending jersey on mountainous stages, suggesting climbing is not a strength.
key_patterns: Won four consecutive sprint stages, demonstrating dominant form and consistent execution pattern.
Positioned 3rd-7th wheel at 600m mark, trusting team delivery over taking early control.
Self-aware about terrain limitations, acknowledging mountain stages as threat to jersey defense.
form_trajectory: positive: four consecutive stage wins demonstrates peak sprint form and repeatable winning execution in 2017 Tour de Pologne
